# Subprocess and Process Security

Reviewable security rules for subprocess execution and localhost services.

Every rule in this document is detectable during code review — if a reviewer
can't grep for it or spot it in a diff, it doesn't belong here.

## Rule 1: Never Use `shell=True`

Always use `shell=False` (the default) when calling `subprocess.run()`,
`subprocess.Popen()`, and related functions. If you need to parse a command
string, use `shlex.split()` instead.

### Why

`shell=True` passes the command through `/bin/sh`, which enables shell
injection if any part of the command comes from user input or external data.
Even when the command is fully controlled, `shell=True` adds an unnecessary
attack surface.

### Examples

```python
import subprocess
import shlex

# Wrong — shell injection risk
subprocess.run(f"convert {input_file} {output_file}", shell=True)

# Right — list of arguments, no shell
subprocess.run(["convert", input_file, output_file])

# Right — parsing a command string safely
cmd = "ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -c:v libx264 output.mp4"
subprocess.run(shlex.split(cmd))
```

### What to Look For in Review

- `shell=True` in any `subprocess` call
- String arguments (instead of lists) passed to `subprocess.run()` — this
  implicitly requires `shell=True` on some platforms

## Rule 2: Bind Localhost Servers to `127.0.0.1`

When running a local HTTP server (for CLI wrappers, dev tools, or any
localhost-only service), always bind to `127.0.0.1`, never `0.0.0.0`.

### Why

Binding to `0.0.0.0` exposes the server on all network interfaces, including
external ones. Localhost is **not** a security boundary — DNS rebinding attacks
can reach services bound to `0.0.0.0` from a remote browser
(CVE-2025-49596 demonstrated RCE via this vector).

### Examples

```python
# Wrong — exposed on all interfaces
uvicorn.run(app, host="0.0.0.0", port=8080)

# Right — localhost only
uvicorn.run(app, host="127.0.0.1", port=8080)
```

```python
# Wrong
server = HTTPServer(("", 8080), handler)

# Right
server = HTTPServer(("127.0.0.1", 8080), handler)
```

### What to Look For in Review

- `host="0.0.0.0"` or `host=""` in any server binding
- `("", port)` tuples in socket or HTTP server constructors
